briefdescription.para: Quarter-sine lookup table for N=256 FFT (Q16 fixed-point).
Reverse the low bits of a value.
Compute twiddle factor (cos, -sin) for a butterfly stage.

detaileddescription.para:
k
Butterfly index within group (0 <= k < m/2).
stage
Current FFT stage (1-based).
wr
[out] Twiddle real (cosine).
wi
[out] Twiddle imaginary (-sine).
The Cooley-Tukey butterfly calls this with k < 2^(stage-1), so idx = (256*k) >> stage is always < 128. Only quadrants 0 and 1 of the sine table are reachable.

briefdescription.para: Computes the in-place Radix-2 Decimation-in-Time FFT.

detaileddescription.para: n must be a power of 2 (e.g., 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256). Max supported size is 256.

parameterdescription.para: Size of the FFT (must be power of 2).

simplesect.para: SYN_OK on success, SYN_ERROR on invalid parameters (e.g. not power of 2).
SYN_OK on success, SYN_INVALID_PARAM if NULL or n <= 1.
SYN_OK on success, SYN_INVALID_PARAM if NULL or n <= 1.
SYN_OK on success, SYN_INVALID_PARAM if NULL or n <= 1.

memberdef.argsstring: (const q16_t *real, const q16_t *imag, q16_t *mag, uint16_t n)
memberdef.name: syn_fft_magnitude_spectrum

briefdescription.para: Compute magnitude spectrum: mag[k] = sqrt(real[k]^2 + imag[k]^2).

simplesect.para: SYN_OK on success, SYN_INVALID_PARAM on invalid inputs.

memberdef.argsstring: (const q16_t *mag, uint16_t n_bins, q16_t sample_rate_hz, SYN_FFTPeak *peaks, uint8_t max_peaks, uint8_t *num_peaks_found)
memberdef.name: syn_fft_find_peaks

briefdescription.para: Find dominant peaks in magnitude spectrum.

simplesect.para: SYN_OK on success, SYN_INVALID_PARAM on invalid inputs.

memberdef.argsstring: (const q16_t *mag, uint16_t n_bins, uint16_t fundamental_bin, uint8_t max_harmonics, q16_t *thd_pct)
memberdef.name: syn_fft_thd

parameterdescription.para: Bin index of the fundamental frequency.
Output THD percentage in Q16.16 (e.g., 5.2% = Q16_FROM_FLOAT(5.2)).

simplesect.para: SYN_OK on success, SYN_INVALID_PARAM on invalid inputs.

briefdescription.para: Fixed-point Q16.16 Radix-2 Decimation-in-Time Fast Fourier Transform implementation.
